de.uka.ilkd.key.settings

Class GeneralSettings

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.settings.GeneralSettings
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Settings, java.lang.Cloneable

    public class GeneralSettings
extends java.lang.Object
implements Settings, java.lang.Cloneable

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static boolean disableSpecs
      if true then JML specifications are globally disabled in this run of KeY, regardless of the regular settings
      static boolean keepFileRepos
      If this option is set, the (Disk)FileRepo does not delete its temporary directories (can be used for debugging).
      static boolean noPruningClosed
      This parameter disables the possibility to prune in closed branches.

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addSettingsListener(SettingsListener l)
      adds a listener to the settings object

      protected void fireSettingsChanged()
      sends the message that the state of this setting has been changed to its registered listeners (not thread-safe)

      Sets the ensureSourceConsistency flag.

      implements the method required by the Settings interface.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • noPruningClosed

        public static boolean noPruningClosed
        This parameter disables the possibility to prune in closed branches. It is meant as a fallback solution if storing all closed goals needs too much memory or is not needed. Pruning is disabled as a default (for command-line mode, tests, ...) and explicitly has to be enabled for interactive mode.

      • keepFileRepos

        public static boolean keepFileRepos
        If this option is set, the (Disk)FileRepo does not delete its temporary directories (can be used for debugging).

      • disableSpecs

        public static boolean disableSpecs
        if true then JML specifications are globally disabled in this run of KeY, regardless of the regular settings

    • Method Detail

      • tacletFilter

        public boolean tacletFilter()

      • isDndDirectionSensitive

        public boolean isDndDirectionSensitive()

      • isRightClickMacro

        public boolean isRightClickMacro()

        public void setDnDDirectionSensitivity(boolean b)

      • setRightClickMacros

        public void setRightClickMacros(boolean b)

      • setUseJML

        public void setUseJML(boolean b)

      • setAutoSave

        public void setAutoSave(int period)

      • setEnsureSourceConsistency

        public void setEnsureSourceConsistency(boolean b)
        Sets the ensureSourceConsistency flag. This enables/disables caching of source files at first use via a FileRepo.
        Parameters:
        b - the new truth value of the flag

      • readSettings

        public void readSettings(java.util.Properties props)
        gets a Properties object and has to perform the necessary steps in order to change this object in a way that it represents the stored settings
        Specified by:
        readSettings in interface Settings

      • writeSettings

        public void writeSettings(java.util.Properties props)
        implements the method required by the Settings interface. The settings are written to the given Properties object. Only entries of the form = (,)* are allowed.
        Specified by:
        writeSettings in interface Settings
        Parameters:
        props - the Properties object where to write the settings as (key, value) pair

      • fireSettingsChanged

        protected void fireSettingsChanged()
        sends the message that the state of this setting has been changed to its registered listeners (not thread-safe)

      • addSettingsListener

        public void addSettingsListener(SettingsListener l)
        adds a listener to the settings object
        Specified by:
        addSettingsListener in interface Settings
        Parameters:
        l - the listener

      • removeSettingsListener

        public void removeSettingsListener(SettingsListener l)
        removes the listener from the settings object
        Specified by:
        removeSettingsListener in interface Settings
        Parameters:
        l - the listener to remove
